title: Inflation: What $1,000 in 1990 Is Worth Today
description: The purchasing power of $1,000 in 1990 relative to 2025, using US CPI-U data. This scenario illustrates why holding cash long-term erodes wealth in real terms.

what_costs_equivalent_today.note: Those items cost ~2.5× more in nominal dollars today.

data_source: US Bureau of Labor Statistics, CPI-U seasonally unadjusted
educational_note: A dollar held as cash in 1990 is worth ~40 cents in purchasing power today. This is why long-term investors seek returns that exceed inflation. The S&P 500's ~10% average annual nominal return, vs ~2.8% average inflation, produces a ~7% real annual return.
